Event: Performs acrobatic tricks and flips during his shows
Explanation: Entertains the audience and sustains his own popularity and career.

Event: Promotes children's literacy on his show while being illiterate himself
Explanation: He meaningfully promotes literacy for kids even though he personally lacks the skill.

Event: Later becomes at least semi-literate
Explanation: Gaining literacy substantially benefits him and mildly helps others via better functioning.

Event: Lights cigars with $100 bills
Explanation: He pointlessly destroys his own money with no effect on others.

Event: Buys a new house because the old one is dirty
Explanation: He gains comfort but wastes huge resources for something that could be cheaply fixed.

Event: Immediately pulls down a map and explains the Falklands invasion to the audience
Explanation: He quickly informs viewers, showcasing knowledge while engaging the audience.

Event: Admits on air that he's illiterate in "Krusty Gets Busted"
Explanation: Honesty risks his image but helps prove his innocence and inform others.

Event: Makes kids cry-laugh after revealing he’s bipolar
Explanation: He channels his condition into performance, coping himself and amusing others.

Event: Dumps a load of snow on Robert Frost during a serious appearance
Explanation: He gets a gag for his show at the expense of a serious guest’s dignity.

Event: Becomes a greedy shameless sellout who licenses his name and likeness for cash
Explanation: He profits heavily from over-licensing while offering low value.

Event: Gets fired for 22 years over stuck stage shutters (after swearing on TV)
Explanation: He suffers massively in career over a relatively minor outburst.

Event: Has his bus pass revoked for drinking a soda
Explanation: He loses mobility over a trivial infraction with no broader benefit.

Event: Switches his show from politics to a kiddie show
Explanation: The move greatly boosts his career and gives enduring entertainment to kids.

Event: Hates being a clown but keeps doing it for the limelight
Explanation: He sacrifices personal satisfaction for fame and an audience that enjoys him.

Event: Reinvents himself as an adult-oriented social comic
Explanation: He revitalizes his act and offers sharper commentary to viewers.

Event: Later rejects the new persona to return to a corporate-sponsored lifestyle
Explanation: He chooses higher income and comfort over less lucrative but richer comedy for others.

Event: Sells his likeness rights internationally
Explanation: He earns by licensing himself even as foreign clones outshine him.

Event: Shills almost anything solely for money
Explanation: He gains income but routinely foists poor-value products on buyers.

Event: Finds no humor in Apu cheating on his wife with many kids involved
Explanation: His disapproval aligns with protecting a large family and reflects some moral sense.

Event: Makes it up to kids from Kamp Krusty by taking them to Tijuana
Explanation: He spends resources to compensate them, regaining goodwill and giving them fun.

Event: Gambles heavily, including wagering against the Harlem Globetrotters
Explanation: His compulsive betting devastates his finances and angers the Mafia without helping others.

Event: Accepts agent’s advice to switch from serious stand-up to cheap laughs
Explanation: He gains more money and fame but lowers artistic and audience value.

Event: Openly admits he would have someone whacked for $1000, then calls it a joke
Explanation: He toys with violent power to impress while unsettling others, gaining only edge and attention.

Event: Treats Sideshow Bob badly, prompting Bob’s revenge
Explanation: He asserts dominance as a mean boss but cultivates a long-term deadly enemy.

Event: Continues mistreating Sideshow Mel and Mr. Teeny
Explanation: He extracts compliance and labor while harming their well-being and morale.

Event: Regularly steels or plagiarizes jokes from other comedians
Explanation: He boosts his material cheaply at the direct creative expense of peers.

Event: Spends millions on extravagant, pointless luxuries
Explanation: His extreme conspicuous consumption repeatedly drains his own wealth.

Event: Endorses dangerous and untested products without checking them
Explanation: He profits from slapping his brand on items that can seriously harm buyers.

Event: Allows Krusty-O cereal with jagged metal to reach children
Explanation: He gains sales while subjecting kids to serious injury risks.

Event: Replaces Krusty-O metal hazard with flesh-eating bacteria after lawsuit
Explanation: He keeps profiting from a product that’s lethally unsafe, risking lawsuits and brand damage.

Event: Sells pregnancy tests that can induce birth defects
Explanation: He profits in the short term while causing catastrophic harm and eventual backlash.

Event: Operates unsanitary Krusty Burger restaurants
Explanation: He cuts corners for profit but endangers customers’ health.

Event: Mass-merchandises everything with low-quality goods
Explanation: He makes money off shoddy, often unsafe items that disappoint or hurt buyers.

Event: Shifts from caring clown to indifferent sellout who doesn’t care about kids
Explanation: He retains income but neglects their welfare and trust.

Event: Sexually harasses younger women and sidekick Ms. "No Means No"
Explanation: He indulges his desires while seriously harming and endangering the women.

Event: Buys huge quantities of porn, helping Apu’s store stay afloat
Explanation: His vice spending brings him gratification and materially supports Apu’s business.

Event: Takes Sophie into his life but remains a neglectful father
Explanation: He partially engages but sabotages the relationship through neglect, limiting benefits for both.

Event: Forgets Bart’s past help and stays ungrateful
Explanation: He keeps his status while denying recognition and emotional reward to Bart.

Event: Says “When I’m off the clock, kids can go jump in a lake”
Explanation: He maintains boundaries and cynicism but alienates or devalues the very kids who support him.

Event: Deliberately hides his Jewish heritage on camera
Explanation: He may protect marketability but harms his own identity and positive representation.

Event: Self-medicates his depression with alcohol
Explanation: Short-term numbing worsens his long-term mental and physical health.

Event: Regains stardom by returning to clowning after faking his death
Explanation: Coming back restores his career and gives his fans back a beloved performer.

Event: Sometimes goes out of his way to help others when he has some gain
Explanation: He secures mutual benefit, improving his image and genuinely aiding others.

Event: Uses edgy smoking and anti-establishment persona onstage for a time
Explanation: He leverages vices into a style that refreshes his act and entertains adults.

Event: Constantly forgets or dismisses Bart’s efforts as if they never happened
Explanation: He preserves his ego while denying Bart validation and reciprocity.

Event: States he wants to be a clown to make people laugh as a child
Explanation: His core motivation aligns his life pursuit with bringing joy to others.

Event: Starts “Krusty Komedy Klassic” without realizing the KKK acronym
Explanation: His ignorance creates offense and reputational damage with almost no upside beyond another show title.
